name: Tofu Steaks with Pineapple Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1 1-1/4 lb Firm tofu, drained and cut into lengthwise 4 1-inch steaks
recipeInstructions:
1. About 3 1/2 hours before serving: From the bunch of cilantro, remove 4 sprigs to use as garnish later. Chop the remaining cilantro to make 1/2 cup; set aside 1 tablespoon for salsa.
2. In a medium-sized baking dish, combine chopped cilantro and the next six ingredients (white vegetable stock, lemon juice, crushed red pepper, minced ginger root, garlic cloves, black pepper). Add tofu. Marinate for 2 hours at room temperature.
3. About 1-1/4 hours before serving, peel the pineapple and finely chop it. In a serving bowl, combine the chopped pineapple, jalapeno pepper, and reserved 1 tablespoon chopped cilantro.
4. Prepare an outdoor grill by lightly oiling it.
5. Place tofu on the grill over medium heat; reserve the marinade. Grill tofu until lightly browned, brushing frequently with the marinade and turning once with a pancake turner.
6. Serve tofu with the pineapple mixture. Garnish with cilantro and avocado if desired. Makes 4 servings.
TO BROIL IN OVEN:
Follow steps 1 through 3. Preheat the broiler. Lightly oil the rack in the broiling pan; place tofu on the rack; broil for about 6-8 minutes until lightly browned, brushing frequently with the marinade and turning once with a pancake turner.
